papers.name: Multiple-Choice Questions
papers.coverage_notes: 40 discrete multiple-choice questions covering all 8 units and science practices.

papers.name: Free-Response Questions
papers.coverage_notes: 4 free-response questions: Mathematical Routines, Translation Between Representations, Experimental Design and Analysis, Qualitative/Quantitative Translation.

assessment_objectives.name: Creating Representations
assessment_objectives.description: Create representations of physical situations using diagrams, graphs, or equations.

assessment_objectives.name: Mathematical Routines
assessment_objectives.description: Use qualitative and quantitative reasoning, calculate, compare, estimate, and predict functional dependence.

assessment_objectives.name: Scientific Questioning and Argumentation
assessment_objectives.description: Identify experimental procedures, recognize patterns and trends, and develop scientific arguments.

difficulty_ladder.description: single concept recall or direct application (e.g., state a definition, calculate a single kinematic quantity)
multi-step application within one unit (e.g., apply Newton's second law with friction and solve for acceleration)
cross-unit reasoning or exam-style synthesis (e.g., combine energy conservation with momentum in a collision, or design and justify an experiment)

lesson_use.notes: v1 skill supports classification and qbank routing only. Teacher/student/parent modes are not enabled.

exclude_from_questions: AP Physics 2 topics: thermodynamics, electrostatics, circuits, magnetism, optics, modern physics

marking_guidance.notes: Task verbs used in free-response questions are listed in the CED p.182. Use generated marking guidance only; do not copy official mark schemes.
